summary: The ideal client is a founder-led UK service business at £2m–£10m revenue where the founder is still the operational bottleneck.
jtbd_statement: Get me in front of founder-MDs who are stuck running everything so I can fill the pipeline without doing the outreach myself.

tier_1.label: Ideal Client
tier_1.description: Founder-MD of a UK B2B service business at £3m–£12m revenue who is personally stuck in delivery, has no real management layer, and has already tried to fix it themselves.

company_profile.stage: Past early growth, hitting the complexity wall.

buyer_profile.title: Founder / Managing Director
buyer_profile.seniority: Sole decision-maker. No board, no investors with veto power.
buyer_profile.day_to_day: In every room. Every decision routes through them.
buyer_profile.identity: They see themselves as a builder, not a manager.

push: The founder has not taken a proper holiday in over a year.
pull: A management team that runs the weekly rhythm without the founder in the room.
anxiety: They have been burned by consultants before.
habit: Referrals keep arriving just often enough to remove the acute pain.

triggers.trigger: A key senior person has left in the last 90 days.

disqualifiers: Founder is not the sole decision-maker.

tier_2.label: Good Client
tier_2.description: Founder-led UK service businesses at £1.5m–£3m or £12m–£20m that fit the profile but sit outside the sweet spot.

company_profile.stage: Approaching or past the complexity wall.

buyer_profile.title: Founder / Managing Director, occasionally a co-founder pair
buyer_profile.seniority: Decision-maker or one of two decision-makers.
buyer_profile.day_to_day: Smaller end: doing everything. Larger end: pulled back in despite trying to step away.
buyer_profile.identity: Similar to Tier 1 but with key difference on urgency.

push: Smaller end: founder knows the model breaks at 20 staff.
pull: Smaller end: idea of having someone who has done this before.
anxiety: Can they afford £5k a month when cash is tight?
habit: The founder can still do everything personally.

triggers.trigger: Founder has just lost a key client or had a quarter where revenue dropped 15%.

switching_costs: Smaller end: the £12k diagnostic is a larger proportion of available cash.

tier_3.label: Do Not Target
tier_3.description: Engaging these businesses wastes pipeline budget and risks failed engagements.

company_profile.stage: Pre-product-market-fit or mature enough to need a full-time COO.
company_profile.business_model: SaaS, product-led, e-commerce, or any non-service business.

buyer_profile.title: Non-founder operators or early-stage solopreneurs
buyer_profile.day_to_day: Running a team too small for PartScale's intervention.
buyer_profile.identity: First-time founder or professional manager who sees fractional COOs as a cost.

push: Sub-£1m founders feel overwhelmed but the real problem is unproven business model.
pull: Attracted to stepping back, but they are the delivery.
habit: The founder is addicted to doing the work.

triggers.trigger: A sub-£1m founder reaches out after reading a LinkedIn post.

switching_costs: For PartScale, the cost is reputational.
